sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
The patient reports back pain for approximately one week.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports back pain for approximately one week, with a history of twisting his left side approximately a week prior to the onset of pain. The patient's pain returned after a few hours following the initial twist. The patient's pain is located in the center of his back, with some radiation to the lower back and occasional pain on the left side. The patient's pain is exacerbated by sudden movements to the left. The patient tried over-the-counter medications but they did not alleviate the pain. He is currently taking metformin twice a day and tests his blood sugar at home most mornings, with slightly elevated levels.
Findings
The patient's a1c has not been tested in approximately five months. The clinician ordered an a1c test.
Assessment
The clinician suspects a musculoskeletal low back strain.
Plan
The clinician ordered a low back x-ray. The clinician ordered physical therapy. The clinician prescribed 400 milligrams of ibuprofen. The clinician prescribed flexeril. The clinician advises caution with flexeril before strenuous activities. The clinician ordered an a1c test. The patient should check his blood sugars every morning upon waking.
